claim_status: Task queue and handoff organization only; no task is approved, running, accepted, release-gated, or allowed to make compatibility, security, performance, memory, energy, Chrome-class, or production claims.

preconditions: PB-002 remains blocked and ADR-0009 remains unresolved
source-strategy owner identifies candidate source baselines or explicitly records that no baseline is selected
no Servo-derived or other browser-engine code enters a release path
acceptance_criteria: ADR9-EV-001 through ADR9-EV-018 each have owner-reviewed evidence, an explicit missing output, or a recorded blocker

first real ADR-0009 decision review starts from the checked no-claim decision-review template and replaces null selected option, source baseline, feature profile, owner reviewer, independent reviewer, and false decision status flags with owner-reviewed evidence or explicit blocked status
selected source baseline and provenance policy are recorded or the lack of selection is explicitly retained

license, advisory, SBOM, native package, generated-output, unsafe, FFI, local compatibility corpus HTTPS/browser execution beyond the checked harness plan and WPT/Test262 denominator, performance, security, maintenance, and public-claim impacts are synchronized
ADR-0009 decision draft states accepted, rejected, or still-blocked status without promoting unsupported claims
validation passes without weakening any source-strategy gate
negative_tests: no release code imports Chromium, WebKit, Gecko, Servo, Electron, CEF, system webview, or remote-rendering code

do not cite the checked no-claim decision-review template as an accepted ADR, source baseline, source adoption, component approval, release-code authorization, PB-002 readiness promotion, or implementation evidence
no dependency, component, compatibility, security, performance, memory, or release claim is introduced by evidence presence alone

rollback.strategy: Revert only the task-specific documentation and registry diffs if owner review rejects the source-strategy packet.

prohibited_paths: unreviewed toolchain installers committed to the repository
preconditions: checked no-claim fresh-host reproduction inventory, run-record template, and fresh-host readiness-review template remain current
fresh reference host or owner-approved clean VM is available
task records exact OS, shell, Rust, Cargo, Git, cache, target directory, source-tree cleanliness, and environment facts
all generated caches and build outputs stay outside durable source

checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template cannot be cited as owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness review, PB-009 readiness, release confidence, production readiness, implementation, or Chrome-class evidence
acceptance_criteria: fresh-host reproduction inventory, run-record template, and fresh-host readiness-review record are updated with the accepted evidence location and no-claim boundary
fresh-host bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask output are recorded with dates, exact OS, shell, Rust, Cargo, Git, and host configuration
cache and target-directory behavior is documented and source-tree cleanliness is verified
owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness review beyond the checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template accepts or rejects PB-009 readiness promotion
pre-build readiness and operating board distinguish reproduced evidence from current-host assumptions
validation passes on the repository after the evidence is linked
negative_tests: do not install untracked dependencies into the repository
do not treat same-host reruns as fresh-host reproduction
do not accept an owner-approved clean VM without explicit waiver and expiry
do not promote PB-009 to ready without independent reproduction evidence

do not cite the checked no-claim fresh-host readiness-review template as owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness review, PB-009 readiness, release confidence, production readiness, implementation, or Chrome-class evidence

rollback.strategy: Remove the reproduction report and revert readiness wording if the run cannot be reproduced.

prohibited_paths: renderer HTML, CSS, DOM, or JavaScript implementation
unsafe code without ledger and review
preconditions: PB-009 fresh-host reproduction is complete or explicitly waived by an owner
checked no-claim IPC capability boundary inventory remains current
checked no-claim IPC schema-source template remains current
checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template remains current but cannot be cited as owner-reviewed IPC readiness
process roles and IPC invariants remain deny-by-default
malformed and oversized messages remain hostile input
acceptance_criteria: canonical IPC schema or generator proposal is linked to process-capability records and starts from the checked no-claim IPC schema-source template
negative tests cover malformed, oversized, stale, duplicate, reordered, unauthorized, wrong-principal, timeout, and cancellation cases
bounded queue and backpressure behavior is specified and tested

owner-reviewed IPC readiness review exists beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template before PB-011 readiness, renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, or implementation claims
documentation and machine records agree on role authority and failure behavior
negative_tests: message parsing cannot silently accept unknown authority expansion
checked IPC schema-source template cannot be cited as schema-generator approval

checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template cannot be cited as owner-reviewed IPC readiness, PB-011 readiness, renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, or implementation evidence
page content, DevTools, extension, or agent input cannot mint capabilities
test coverage must include rejection paths, not only successful messages
timeout and cancellation behavior cannot hang, leak authority, or hide resource ownership

rollback.strategy: Revert task-scoped crate and document changes if IPC authority or compatibility assumptions are rejected.

preconditions: TASK-000003 authority and IPC boundaries are reviewed or explicitly scoped out
checked no-claim sandbox probe inventory remains current
checked no-claim sandbox probe-package template remains current
checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template remains current but cannot be cited as owner-reviewed sandbox readiness
checked no-claim stable operation catalog and evidence schema remain current
target platform and sandbox primitive are named
negative probes are safe to run on the host
acceptance_criteria: checked no-claim sandbox probe inventory remains synchronized with process-capability roles, required surfaces, and unsupported boundaries
versioned no-claim operation catalog, evidence schema, and sandbox contract validator remain synchronized with this task before executable work starts
first executable package starts from the checked no-claim sandbox probe-package template and replaces template-only fields with real run evidence
packaged probe harness records expected-deny file, socket, process, registry, device, shared-memory, credential, debug, profile, and IPC access cases
an unsandboxed control run proves that the harness observes allowed control operations before expected-deny results are accepted
unsupported platform primitives are recorded separately and never counted as passes
application-level stub denial is rejected as sandbox evidence unless operating-system, broker, or launch-policy enforcement is captured
renderer, network, storage, GPU, decoder, extension, DevTools, agent, and updater roles have explicit probe coverage or unsupported notes
sandbox evidence distinguishes design from actual platform-enforced behavior and includes effective platform policy capture
host-safe fixtures, fake credentials, fake profiles, bounded temporary roots, and cleanup checks prevent probe execution from damaging the developer host

owner-reviewed sandbox readiness review exists beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template before PB-012 readiness, sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing safety, SEC-GATE, production-safety, or implementation claims
security docs, risk records, and pre-build readiness agree on remaining gaps
negative_tests: the no-claim sandbox probe-package template cannot be cited as SEC-GATE evidence or probe execution approval

the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template cannot be cited as owner-reviewed sandbox readiness, PB-012 readiness, sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, SEC-GATE, production-safety, or implementation evidence
the no-claim operation catalog and evidence schema cannot be cited as execution approval or platform containment evidence
a successful demo cannot bypass sandbox denial checks
an application-level stub cannot be counted as an operating-system sandbox denial
untrusted input cannot broaden probe authority
no failure is swallowed as a pass

rollback.strategy: Remove probe tooling and revert sandbox status if any probe grants more authority than specified.

prohibited_paths: network downloads during benchmark runs unless explicitly profiled
comparison runs with unequal security, workload, lifecycle, or discard settings
preconditions: current no-claim hardware, OS-control, corpus, network-profile, and resource-attribution registries validate
browser target, prototype target, or explicit no-browser status is declared
checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template remains current and cannot be cited as owner-reviewed benchmark readiness
claim metadata remains no_claim until real owner-reviewed results exist
acceptance_criteria: browser-launch or explicit no-browser runner contract includes timeout, repetitions, cache/profile reset, temporary-profile isolation, prohibited-path checks, viewport, trace capture, failure capture, cancellation, cleanup, and artifact hashing
raw artifact package includes manifests, hashes, logs, and unsupported behavior
resource_attribution_id is carried through runner output and raw artifacts
competitor_version_manifest_id is carried as release-catalog candidate evidence until benchmark-ready local installed-browser pins exist
competitor_local_install_manifest_id is carried as executable/hash candidate evidence until browser-reported versions, profiles, settings, command lines, and channel proof exist

browser_pin_capture_plan_id and browser_pin_diagnostic_id are carried as candidate evidence until owner-reviewed browser pins prove isolated browser-reported versions, effective settings, channel proof, and no real profile access
expanded generated no-claim corpus cases are carried as planning evidence until a reviewed representative corpus, disabled-case denominator, and browser-run artifacts exist

checked runner-managed server lifecycle self-test is carried as planning evidence until browser-run server evidence, benchmark-run server artifacts, DNS execution proof, and richer network profiles exist

checked no-claim 30-tab mixed and all-live scenario manifests are carried as planning evidence until runner-generated raw artifacts, revival measurements, process topology, and state-loss evidence exist
checked no-claim trace and artifact package contract is carried as planning evidence until runner-generated trace packages, redaction review, raw samples, and retention decisions exist

checked no-claim browser launch-runner contract and checked no-browser browser launch-runner self-test are carried as planning evidence until real browser-run artifacts, timeout/cancellation handling, cache/profile reset, and result finalization exist
checked no-claim claim-bundle template is carried as planning evidence until owner-reviewed public-claim evidence from real raw artifacts exists

checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template is carried as planning evidence until owner-reviewed benchmark readiness review from real hardware, corpus, runner, raw artifact, browser-pin, statistics, denominator, and claim-bundle evidence exists
validation rejects any benchmark artifact that omits no-claim or unsupported-behavior metadata
negative_tests: runner cannot produce an approved claim from smoke or sample data
runner cannot hide failures, unknown resource buckets, tab discards, or site-isolation state
competitor comparison remains blocked without pinned versions and equal-workload evidence
public claim bundle remains rejected without raw samples, trace package, statistics, denominator records, owner review, and expiry

checked no-claim benchmark readiness-review template cannot be cited as owner-reviewed benchmark readiness, benchmark-ready status, public performance, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, Chrome-class, competitor-result, daily-driver, production, or implementation evidence

rollback.strategy: Revert runner and benchmark-registry changes if they allow unsupported measurement or claim semantics.

prohibited_paths: runtime React or JavaScript in trusted chrome
runtime CSS parser in trusted chrome release path
preconditions: TASK-000003 IPC and command authority are reviewed or the shell prototype is isolated

checked no-claim adapter contract inventory is treated as planning evidence until ADR-0013, complete toolkit-neutral state, command, surface, accessibility, diagnostic, and adapter contracts, native adapter prototype, no-toolkit-owned-authority negative tests, and owner review exist

checked no-claim framework bake-off inventory is treated as planning evidence until three-adapter or owner-approved reduced reference-shell evidence, ADR-0014, license/provenance review, accessibility evidence, IME/keyboard evidence, page-surface evidence, crash/GPU-loss evidence, and performance evidence exist

checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template is treated as planning evidence only and cannot be cited for toolkit selection, accessibility readiness, page-surface approval, release-path UI approval, or production readiness
acceptance_criteria: checked no-claim adapter contract inventory is treated as planning evidence until ADR-0013, toolkit-neutral state, command, surface, accessibility, diagnostic, and adapter contract evidence, native adapter prototype evidence, no toolkit-owned authority negative tests, and owner review exist

page-surface composition, design-token and component fixtures, accessibility, IME, keyboard, crash, GPU-loss, startup, memory, binary, latency, frame-pacing, energy, license, dependency, and provenance evidence requirements are explicit

checked no-claim window/input/accessibility workflow inventory is treated as planning evidence until executable reference-platform workflow matrix, page-tree composition, input, IME, keyboard, clipboard, drag-drop, localization, zoom, high contrast, reduced motion, manual assistive-technology, screen-reader, latency, crash, renderer-hang, GPU-loss, UI-GATE-7, and UI-GATE-10 evidence exists

checked no-claim page-surface composition inventory is treated as planning evidence until executable resize, scale, damage, input, IME, accessibility, occlusion, capture, renderer-crash, GPU-loss, software fallback, latency, frame-pacing, typed page-surface handles, brokered surface handle, document and device generations, UI-GATE-7, ADR-0016, and owner-review evidence exists

checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template is treated as planning evidence until a real owner-reviewed native UI readiness review replaces null reviewers, selected toolkit, adapter strategy, and reference-platform fields with evidence beyond the template

no toolkit is promoted without ADR and license/provenance review
negative_tests: trusted chrome cannot depend on a webview, Electron, Tauri, system webview, Node, runtime React, runtime JavaScript, runtime DOM, or runtime CSS parser
native adapter prototype success cannot be claimed before ADR-0013, explicit adapter contract evidence, and no toolkit-owned authority negative tests exist
framework bake-off success cannot be claimed from a single adapter, empty-window demo, qualitative source observation, or unchecked license/provenance assumption

UI success cannot hide missing keyboard, screen-reader, manual assistive-technology, IME, resize, scale, damage, occlusion, capture, crash, renderer-hang, GPU-loss, software fallback, typed page-surface handle, brokered surface handle, document and device generations, page-tree, page-surface, latency, frame-pacing, energy, or memory behavior

checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template cannot be cited as toolkit selection, accessibility readiness, page-surface approval, trusted-chrome readiness, release-path UI approval, TASK-000006 execution approval, or production readiness
adapter-specific state cannot become the product source of truth

rollback.strategy: Keep adapter prototypes isolated and revert any toolkit-specific source if review rejects its authority, license, or runtime model.

prohibited_paths: code ownership changes without named reviewers

prohibited_paths: agent-decided vulnerability severity or disclosure timing
